questions.prompt_html:
A dish with a temperature of 54°C is placed in a fridge, which has a constant temperature of 6°C.

Assume Newton's Law of Cooling.
questions.prompt_text: A dish with a temperature of 54°C is placed in a fridge, which has a constant temperature of 6°C. If the dish cools to 30°C in a half hour, what is its temperature after another half hour? Assume Newton's Law of Cooling.
questions.solution_html:
Let y = y(t) be the temperature of the dish (in degrees Celsius) at time t (in hours since the dish was placed in the fridge).
Thus y(0) = 54 and y() = 30. 
Therefore, by Newton's Law of Cooling,  = k(y - m), where m is the temperature of the fridge and k is a constant. Here m = 6, hence  = k(y -6). Now let u = u(t) = y -6 and noting that  = , we obtain the simple equation  = ku. By inspection u = Aekt, for some constant A. Recall u = y -6, hence y -6 = Aekt or y = Aekt + 6.
There remains to find the constants A and k. Applying y(0) = 54, we obtain 54 = Ae0 + 6, which implies 54 = A + 6, or A =54 - 6 = 48. It follows that y = 48 ekt + 6 ........................... (*)
Next, applying y() = 30, we obtain 
30 = 48 ek/2 + 6, so 30 - 6 = 48 ek/2, or ek/2 =

questions.prompt_html:
A tank contains 200 litres of fluid in which 60 grams of salt is dissolved. A brine solution of 2 grams of salt per litre is pumped into the tank at a rate of 2 litres/minute. The well-stirred solution is then pumped out at the same rate.
(a) Write the differential equation in terms of Q(t) and t and write the initial condition to model this problem. (Q(t) should represent the amount of salt in the tank at time t.)
(b) Solve the equation in part (a).
(c) How much salt will be present in the tank after a long period of time?
questions.prompt_text: A tank contains 200 litres of fluid in which 60 grams of salt is dissolved. A brine solution of 2 grams of salt per litre is pumped into the tank at a rate of 2 litres/minute. (Q(t) should represent the amount of salt in the tank at time t.) (b) Solve the equation in part (a). (c) How much salt will be present in the tank after a long period of time?
